### Post by ubuntu irv on 2006-01-19
Please could someone help a newbie...I have a PS2 mouse hooked up to a compaq presario with a touch pad.  I cannot get the mouse to work.

	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/CID"

	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"

	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ImPS/2"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"

	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizScrollDelta"	"0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"ATI Technologies, Inc. 3D Rage LT Pro (AGP)"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

	Option		"DPMS"

### Post by geraldm on 2007-09-26
This is a guess:

Section "InputDevice"
Identifier "Configured Mouse"
Driver "mouse"
Option "CorePointer"
Option "Device" "/dev/input/mice"

The above line is OK with USB mice, but you are using PS/2, so this might have to be changed.
Possibly it should point to a serial device.  Maybe search for a different line in the forums.
I do not know what would work there.  Try "/dev/psaux" ?

Gerald

---

### Post by Linicks on 2007-09-26
Try changing:

```
Option "Protocol" "ImPS/2"
```

to:

```
Option "Protocol" "Auto"
```

Nick
